exemplar.title: To what extent can we better improve the efficiency of polycrystalline solar panels in converting sunlight into electricity?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can we better improve the efficiency of polycrystalline solar panels in converting sunlight into electricity?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated with sufficient clarity and a clear purpose, with background, rationale and objective statement guiding the reader.
Research question is stated and connected to the investigation, showing reasonable focus.
Methodology is generally complete, with appropriate selection of methods and relevant treatment of uncertainties.
weaknesses: Research question remains somewhat broad and could more precisely reference dust accumulation to improve focus.
Controls for irradiance and panel temperature are weak and need more explicit treatment to strengthen experimental design.
Repetition and occasional awkward wording in sections slightly detract from overall clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly in the 3â4 band for all three sub-strands. Communication of topic is adequate rather than exemplary, the RQ is clear but partially focused, and the methodology is mostly complete yet lacks some controls. A mid-band score of 4 out of 6 appropriately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Technical terminology (e.g., photovoltaic effect, P-n junction) is used correctly in most instances.
A wide range of recent and reliable sources are cited, demonstrating good subject knowledge and understanding.
Uncertainty propagation and error analysis are handled competently, showing solid grasp of theoretical underpinning.
weaknesses: Occasional inaccuracies (e.g., polycrystalline vs. monocrystalline efficiency, incorrect unit âterawatt per hourâ) limit consistency.
Some data inconsistencies (efficiency ranges) and undefined symbols (Ï for dust impact) reveal gaps in rigor.
Theoretical depth is good but does not reach the sophistication or accuracy required for the top band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands lie in the 3â4 band: knowledge is good with mostly relevant source application and clear understanding, but inaccuracies and occasional misuse of terminology prevent an excellent rating. A score of 4 out of 6 balances the clear strengths against the noted weaknesses.

strengths: Data are systematically analysed with uncertainties clearly presented, linking trends directly to the research question.
Arguments are coherent, with plausible explanations for anomalies and logical connections between results and literature.
Limitations and potential improvements are identified, demonstrating awareness of experimental weaknesses.
weaknesses: Statistical treatment is limited, and key variables (irradiance, temperature) are not quantitatively controlled or evaluated.
Critical evaluation remains descriptive in parts; deeper interrogation of methodology and discussion of alternative interpretations is lacking.
Conclusions could be strengthened by quantifying efficiency loss per gram of dust and providing more robust comparative analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three critical-thinking strands fall in the lower half of the 4â6 band: competent analysis and coherent argument but lacking depth in statistical treatment and critical evaluation. Allocating 7 out of 12 acknowledges solid performance while reflecting room for stronger evaluation and quantitative rigor.

strengths: Clear overall structure with title page, table of contents, headings, tables and figures following standard conventions.
Layout generally supports readability, with figures placed near related text and logical sequencing of sections.
Use of tables and plots (with error bars) enhances data communication and reader comprehension.
weaknesses: Several formatting issues remain (mis-numbered sub-sections, missing page numbers in contents) that impede navigation.
Figure and table captions are sometimes incomplete or inconsistent; missing citation details and scale bars reduce clarity.
Minor typographical errors in captions and uneven decimal alignment in tables suggest need for more careful proofreading.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands are in the 3â4 band: structure and formatting are clear but flawed by minor omissions, and visual layout supports communication yet bears inconsistencies. A score of 3 out of 4 appropriately reflects generally effective presentation tempered by these weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: Scientific terms such as photovoltaic effect, power input/output, uncertainties and inclination angle are generally used correctly.  Occasional misuse ("terawatt per hour", misuse of symbols) prevents full consistency.  Overall accuracy aligns with the 3-4 band.

The discussion relates results to the literature review, identifies an anomaly and offers a plausible explanation, and leads to a reasoned answer.  Logical flow is present but not highly sophisticated; alternative interpretations or quantitative comparisons are sparse.  This again aligns with the lower part of the 4-6 band.

A basic experimental plan, variable table, data-collection method and uncertainty treatment are provided and executed.  Nevertheless, controls for irradiance and panel temperature are weak, source selection is modest, and methodology does not fully address the stated broad RQ.  This indicates a mostly complete but not highly effective method (band 3-4, lower end).

The essay explains the photovoltaic effect, factors influencing efficiency and basic power/efficiency calculations correctly, and cites a range of secondary sources.  However, some inaccuracies remain (e.g., claiming polycrystalline panels are the most efficient) and theoretical depth is limited.  This demonstrates âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ knowledgeâband 3-4.

A limitations section and âpotential improvementsâ show awareness of experimental weaknesses (irradiance variability, panel orientation).  Evaluation is competent but remains brief and mostly descriptive, lacking deeper critique of methodology or literature.  Therefore it belongs to the middle band (4-6) at a score of 4.

The student states a clear overall topicâimproving the efficiency of polycrystalline solar panelsâand introduces it with background, personal engagement and a table of contents.  Although sections occasionally contain repetition and awkward wording, the scientific focus (dust-induced efficiency loss) can be followed without difficulty.  Hence communication is adequate rather than exemplary, aligning with the middle band (3-4).

Tables and graphs are legible and placed near relevant text; images of apparatus aid clarity.  Some captions are incomplete and figure quality varies, yet the layout generally supports communication.  This fits the 3-4 band, lower end.

The research question is stated but remains broad ("To what extent can we better improveâ¦") while the investigation actually narrows to dust accumulation only.  Connection to discussion is present but partial; therefore the work fits the 3-4 band, toward the lower end.

Data are collected systematically, tabulated with uncertainties, and plotted to reveal a trend; analysis is explicitly linked to dust mass.  Yet irradiance variation and temperature effects are not quantitatively treated, and statistical depth is limited.  This fits a âgoodâ (4-6) but not outstanding level, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

The essay contains a title page, abstract-style introduction, table of contents, clear section headings, tables and figures, and stays within the word limit.  Minor formatting flaws (mis-numbered sub-sections, uneven figure labelling) exist but overall structure follows EE conventions, meriting the upper band (3-4) at 3.

annotations.comment: The water usage comparison in panel cleaning remains qualitative; quantify consumption or mention typical volumes for stronger evaluation.
The recommendation to schedule experiments on days with stable weather demonstrates practical insight into reducing measurement uncertainty.

The research question is broadly phrased and does not fully align with the focus on dust accumulation. Consider refining it to explicitly mention dust to improve clarity and connection to the investigation.
The isolated header â5.4 Potential Improvementsâ on this page appears without accompanying discussion; ensure each section heading is immediately followed by relevant content.
The inclusion of the word count at the front meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates compliance with submission guidelines.
Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers, making navigation less effective. Populate all page references to enhance usability.
The table of contents is clearly structured with hierarchical headings that guide the reader effectively.
The student demonstrates genuine personal engagement by linking global energy trends to their motivation for investigating solar-panel efficiency.
The headline âWind and solar added moreâ¦â dominates the introduction unnecessarily; reduce its prominence or integrate a concise summary instead.
The student correctly identifies polycrystalline panels as widely used and outlines efficiencyâcost trade-offs, demonstrating solid subject knowledge.
The claim that polycrystalline panels are the most efficient contradicts industry data showing monocrystalline performance is higher; verify source accuracy.
The narrative explaining panel types effectively uses terminology like wafers and cells, showing appropriate use of concepts.
Unit âterawatt per hourâ is incorrect; use terawatt-hour (TWh) for energy or specify power rate in TW.
Table 1 clearly summarizes efficiency, cost, and formation differences of panel types, aiding reader comprehension.
Figure 2 caption lacks the original publication details or DOI; include complete citation to acknowledge source.
The list of factors affecting efficiency is comprehensive, demonstrating good exploration of variables influencing panel performance.
Efficiency ranges listed here (15â17%) conflict with earlier text; ensure consistency between narrative and table data.
The description of the photovoltaic effect correctly outlines Pân junction operation, reflecting sound theoretical understanding.
The use of Ï to denote dust impact is ambiguous; define this symbol and its physical meaning.
The rationale for choosing dust as the experimental focus connects well to identified knowledge gaps, showing informed method selection.
The âGaps in Current Knowledgeâ paragraph reiterates broad issues but does not explicitly link back to the main research question; deepen this connection.
Figure 3 lacks scale bars and contextual labels; include these for clearer interpretation of the electrostatic cleaning mechanism.
The procedural steps are logically sequenced, explaining how each trial controls variables and collects data.
The procedure omits the total number of replicates per dust level; specify repetitions to support statistical reliability.
The discussion effectively links observed trends back to the research question, reinforcing the studyâs focus.
Future research ideas are diverse but lack prioritization; select one key follow-up experiment for deeper investigation.
The discussion of global significance effectively connects the studyâs implications to broader climate-change goals.
The objective statement is concise and clearly defines the scope of the experiment, supporting focused investigation.
The controlled variables list includes both âsunlightâ and âintensityâ, which overlap; clarify distinct parameters and how each is held constant.
Ensure consistent notation in equations: e.g., use capital P for power input/output throughout to avoid confusion.
Uncertainty propagation for power input is correctly calculated and demonstrates strong understanding of error analysis.
The results table includes uncertainties but uses inconsistent decimal alignment; align decimals for better readability.
The systematic presentation of data with uncertainties provides a robust foundation for subsequent analysis.
Graph 1 caption contains a typo (âvectionâ); revise it to accurately describe the relationship plotted.
Plotting efficiency vs. dust amount with error bars in Excel is effective and demonstrates competence in data visualization.
The anomaly between 6 g and 10 g is attributed to luminosity variation without supportive data; consider additional measurements or controls.
The limitations section overlooks panel temperature control, which can significantly impact efficiency; include this factor in future evaluations.
The conclusion restates findings effectively but would benefit from quantifying the average efficiency loss per gram of dust.
The summary of key findings is coherent and summarizes how dust accumulation reduces panel efficiency.
Citation formats are inconsistent and some entries lack DOIs; adopt a single citation style and verify each reference.
A wide range of sources, including recent journal articles and industry reports, demonstrates thorough research and understanding of the topic.
